criteria_verification.evidence: Added `--mode` Click option to `queue_cmd` in `src/forge/cli/queue.py` with `click.Choice(['a','b','c'], case_sensitive=False)` and `default='a'`. Added module-level `resolve_mode(value)` helper that translates the short flag (a/b/c) into `BuildMode.MODE_A/B/C` (also accepts the long enum-string form). The CLI calls `persistence.queue_build(payload, mode=build_mode)` (with a fallback to `record_pending_build` for in-memory test fakes that pre-date the alias). Verified by `TestQueueModeFlag` (3 parameterised + case-variant tests + default-to-A test + bad-value-rejection test).
criteria_verification.notes: Added `--mode` Click option to `queue_cmd` in `src/forge/cli/queue.py` with `click.Choice(['a','b','c'], case_sensitive=False)` and `default='a'`. Added module-level `resolve_mode(value)` helper that translates the short flag (a/b/c) into `BuildMode.MODE_A/B/C` (also accepts the long enum-string form). The CLI calls `persistence.queue_build(payload, mode=build_mode)` (with a fallback to `record_pending_build` for in-memory test fakes that pre-date the alias). Verified by `TestQueueModeFlag` (3 parameterised + case-variant tests + default-to-A test + bad-value-rejection test).

criteria_verification.criterion_text: `forge queue --mode b <FEAT-ID>` requires exactly one feature
criteria_verification.evidence: Changed `feature_id` argument to `feature_ids` with `nargs=-1, required=True`. Added explicit length check after `resolve_mode()` runs: when `mode=BuildMode.MODE_B` and `len(feature_ids) > 1`, the CLI raises `click.UsageError` citing 'FEAT-FORGE-008 ASSUM-006: single feature per Mode B build' BEFORE any persistence/publish call, so misuse never produces a partial SQLite write. Verified by `TestModeBSingleFeature::test_mode_b_with_two_features_is_rejected_at_parse_time` which asserts `result.exit_code != 0` and `fake_persistence.calls == []` and `captured_publish == []`.

Verified by `TestModeBSingleFeature::test_mode_b_with_two_features_is_rejected_at_parse_time` which asserts `result.exit_code != 0` and `fake_persistence.calls == []` and `captured_publish == []`.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: `forge status` displays the mode column (default-rendered as `mode-a`
criteria_verification.evidence: Updated `src/forge/cli/status.py`: (a) `_row_to_status_view` now reads `row['mode']` with `(IndexError, KeyError)` fallback to `BuildMode.MODE_A` so legacy rows pre-dating the v2 migration render as `mode-a`; (b) both branches of `_query_status_views` SELECT the `mode` column; (c) `_build_table` adds a `MODE` column between FEATURE and STATUS that renders `view.mode.value`; (d) `Console(width=160)` keeps the table from squeezing in non-tty CliRunner contexts. Verified by `TestStatusDisplaysMode` (legacy default-to-mode-a test + concurrent mode-b/mode-c rendering test).

criteria_verification.criterion_text: `forge history --mode b` and `--mode c` filter the history view by
criteria_verification.evidence: Updated `src/forge/cli/history.py`: (a) added `resolve_mode_flag(value)` helper that returns `None` for absent input (= 'no filter'); (b) added `_filter_mode(rows, mode)` Python-side filter mirroring `_filter_since`; (c) extended `fetch_history` and `run_history` with a `mode` kwarg; (d) added `--mode` Click choice to `history_cmd`. Verified by `TestHistoryFiltersByMode`: default (no filter) returns all 3 modes, `--mode b` returns only mode-b row, `--mode c` returns only mode-c row.
criteria_verification.notes: Updated `src/forge/cli/history.py`: (a) added `resolve_mode_flag(value)` helper that returns `None` for absent input (= 'no filter'); (b) added `_filter_mode(rows, mode)` Python-side filter mirroring `_filter_since`; (c) extended `fetch_history` and `run_history` with a `mode` kwarg; (d) added `--mode` Click choice to `history_cmd`. Verified by `TestHistoryFiltersByMode`: default (no filter) returns all 3 modes, `--mode b` returns only mode-b row, `--mode c` returns only mode-c row.

criteria_verification.evidence: Added `pick_next_pending(project=None)` method to `SqliteLifecyclePersistence` in `src/forge/lifecycle/persistence.py`. It composes the existing `SqliteSequentialQueuePicker.next_build_to_pick` (lazy import to avoid circular import) which already uses `ORDER BY queued_at ASC` — there is no mode predicate anywhere in the picker SQL. Verified by `TestPickNextPendingFifoRegardlessOfMode::test_fifo_order_preserved_across_modes` which queues Mode B at T0, Mode A at T1, Mode C at T2 and asserts the picker returns the Mode B build (oldest queued_at) first.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Constitutional gate cannot be bypassed by mode flag (Group E "skip
criteria_verification.evidence: The constitutional gate is keyed off `stage` alone — `ConstitutionalGuard.veto_skip(stage)` ignores mode entirely (see `src/forge/pipeline/constitutional_guard.py`). `PULL_REQUEST_REVIEW` is in `CONSTITUTIONAL_STAGES`. The `forge skip` CLI already routes through this guard via `CliSteeringHandler.handle_skip`. No code change needed beyond verification. Verified by `TestConstitutionalGateNotBypassed` parameterised over MODE_A/MODE_B/MODE_C — each asserts `decision.verdict is SkipVerdict.REFUSED_CONSTITUTIONAL` and rationale cites 'ADR-ARCH-026'.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Help text for `--mode` references the FEAT-FORGE-008 chain shapes
criteria_verification.evidence: `_MODE_HELP_TEXT` constant in `src/forge/cli/queue.py` reproduces all three chain shapes verbatim: Mode A's eight stages (`product-owner -> architect -> system-arch -> system-design -> feature-spec -> task-review -> autobuild -> pull-request-review`), Mode B's start-at-/feature-spec / skip-list (ASSUM-001), and Mode C's /task-review + /task-work pairing with optional pull-request-review terminator (ASSUM-004). issues.description:
Advisory (non-blocking): task-work produced a report with 2 of 3 expected agent invocations. Missing phases: 3 (Implementation). Consider invoking these agents via the Task tool to strengthen stack-specific quality:
- Phase 3: `python-api-specialist` (Implementation)

rationale: All quality gates passed. Independent verification confirmed. All acceptance criteria met. Architecture context: 1477 chars provided.
context_used:
## Job-Specific Context

Budget: 2006/7892 tokens

### 🎨 Relevant Patterns
- ROUTES_TO
- APPLIES_TO
- HAS_FALLBACK_STRATEGY
- CREATED_AT

### ⚠️ Warnings
- HAS_ROOT_CAUSE
- IS_IMPLEMENTED_AS
- INCORRECT_MODE_BEHAVIOR
- REQUIRES_USE_OF

### 🎭 Role Constraints
- MUST_CONSULT_BEFORE_MODIFYING
- DELEGATE_TO_TASK_WORK
- DELEGATES_TO
- MONITORS

### 🛠️ Implementation Modes
- APPLIES_TO_TASK
- APPLIES_TO

  • `forge queue --mode c <SUBJECT-ID>` accepts a subject identifier (e.g.
